mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces borrowing costs for corporations and households, directly boosting equity valuations through lower discount rates and higher expected earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which compounds to drive positive equity returns over a quarter as corporate revenues accelerate across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at p < 0.05 in out-of-sample regression).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high industrial production growth, positive month-over-month changes in the index of industrial production signal robust supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns during high-growth expansions (Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect consistent job creation, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and suppresses forward returns over the next quarter due to higher discount rates and reduced liquidity.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes > 0.4% are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains negative over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly auto sales, where auto sales decelerate while GDP remains elevated, signals a shift in consumer spending patterns that negatively impacts cyclical equity weights, producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence interaction term shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, a decline in industrial production indicates that monetary tightening is successfully slowing economic activity, which reduces near-term earnings growth and drives negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-rate regimes (>3.0%) are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
